output.summary: Scope the ResizeObserver in Hero.js to only target .top-gradient elements inside .hero block wrappers, preventing conflicts with other blocks that also use a .top-gradient class.

fileChanges.description: Replace the global document.querySelectorAll('.top-gradient') selector with a scoped approach that finds .top-gradient elements only within .hero wrappers.
fileChanges.details: Change line 6 from `document.querySelectorAll('.top-gradient')` to `document.querySelectorAll('.hero .top-gradient')`. This ensures the ResizeObserver only attaches to gradient elements that are children of the Hero block's wrapper element (which has the class 'hero' applied via theme_block_props in Hero.php). No other changes are needed -- the rest of the logic (finding the closest <main>, observing resize, setting maxHeight) remains identical.

designDecisions.decision: Use '.hero .top-gradient' descendant selector to scope the query
designDecisions.evidence: Hero.php applies the 'hero' class to the block wrapper via theme_block_props (line 8-14). The .top-gradient div is a direct child of that wrapper (line 18). Using a descendant selector is the simplest scoping mechanism and requires no PHP changes.
